No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to ending hunger or improving nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to health or well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly develops problem-solving skills for employment (Target 4.4) and fosters understanding of sustainability issues (Target 4.7), making a strong, direct contribution to quality education.
evidence: Students will acquire problem solving skills.
Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to energy access or renewables.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops problem-solving skills and the ability to create actionable marketing solutions, which contribute to higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).
evidence: Students will acquire problem solving skills.
develop practical and actionable marketing solutions

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches fundamental research methods and 'Marketing Analytics and AI', directly enhancing scientific research and innovation capabilities (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly teaches students to identify sustainability issues, directly supporting sustainable practices (Target 12.6) and fostering awareness for sustainable consumption (Target 12.8) in a marketing context.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course lacks specific content directly addressing climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to marine ecosystems or resources.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's emphasis on understanding ethics and social responsibility can indirectly contribute to fostering accountable practices within future institutions (Target 16.6).
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course emphasizes data analysis, statistical interpretation, and fact-based decision-making, directly supporting the availability of high-quality data and measurement of progress for the SDGs (Targets 17.18, 17.19).
evidence: analyze a given data set and interpret the results independently
